analysis.overall_assessment: The single detected tea leaf shows severe discoloration and necrotic damage, indicating critical health deterioration. No healthy portions are visible, rendering the leaf unsuitable for standard tea production.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Install windbreak barriers (e.g., bamboo screens) around tea bushes to reduce direct pollutant exposure
Apply calcium-based foliar sprays to neutralize acidic pollutants on leaf surfaces
Transition to low-sulfur fuel sources for nearby machinery and collaborate with local industries to reduce SO₂ emissions

immediate_actions: Isolate and discard all visibly damaged leaves to prevent contamination of healthy batches
Conduct pH testing of soil and leaf samples to confirm sulfur compound accumulation
Sanitize processing equipment to avoid cross-contamination
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Reject all leaves from this batch; implement optical sorting technology in future harvests to detect necrotic spots at early stages
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Not suitable for orthodox or CTC tea processing; consider experimental extraction for non-beverage applications only
quality_preservation: Store healthy leaves (if any in future harvests) in airtight containers with oxygen absorbers
Process healthy leaves within 24 hours of harvest to minimize oxidation

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: None - the leaf is fully necrotic with no viable tissue
alternative_uses: Use as biomass for biochar production to improve soil structure
Extract tannins for natural dye applications in textile industries
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Layer with 3:1 ratio of green waste (e.g., grass clippings) to balance carbon content; maintain 55-65°C for 14 days to neutralize pollutants
prevention_measures: Plant pollution-tolerant cover crops like clover between tea rows
Implement real-time air quality monitoring with SO₂ sensors at canopy height
Rotate tea cultivars to SO₂-resistant varieties such as 'TRF 1' or 'S15/10'

cost_saving_opportunities: Redirect rejected leaves to low-cost biomass energy projects
Claim insurance coverage for pollution-related crop damage
Partner with research institutions for pollutant remediation grants
value_recovery_methods: Convert to organic compost for sale to neighboring farms
Extract residual polyphenols for cosmetic industry applications

priority_actions: Deploy SO₂ air quality monitors within 72 hours to establish baseline pollution levels
Install windbreak barriers within 2 weeks before next monsoon season
Conduct soil remediation with lime application to neutralize sulfur compounds within 30 days
recommendations.timeline: Immediate (0-7 days) for monitoring, short-term (2-4 weeks) for barriers, medium-term (1-3 months) for soil remediation
monitoring_points: Daily SO₂ concentration levels at 1m height
Weekly leaf tissue analysis for sulfur accumulation
Monthly soil pH and sulfate content measurements
